message:
feat(tests): add progress_tests module to conformance suite

- Add progress_tests to conformance/mod.rs exports
- Enhance progress_tests.rs with additional markup parsing tests
- Close bd-24gn (Progress bar description markup tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -87,13 +87,14 @@
 {"id":"bd-1vqq","title":"Fresh-eyes code exploration + bug audit pass (random modules)","description":"Goal: randomly sample core rendering modules, trace execution flows, and fix any obvious bugs found. Scope: Console/Segment/Text/Style pipeline and one renderable (Table/Panel/Progress). Method: read code paths end-to-end, cross-check invariants (width, newline handling, style transitions), then add targeted fixes/tests. Deliverables: bug fixes with tests or snapshot updates; notes in bead comments for findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:39:52.385060185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:44:56.350960874Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:44:56.350942369Z","close_reason":"Fix Columns padding overflow on narrow widths; add test; run fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1wk","title":"PrintOptions.soft_wrap has no behavioral effect","description":"PrintOptions::soft_wrap only forces width selection in Console::print_to; no soft-wrapping behavior is implemented in Text/Console rendering. Decide expected semantics and implement or remove flag.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:10:11.570072666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:48:29.086509996Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:48:29.086425517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-21uy","title":"Panel markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Panel titles and content may have similar markup parsing issues as Table cells. Need explicit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Panel::new() with markup in title - verify markup is parsed
- [ ] Test Panel with markup in content - verify markup is parsed  
- [ ] Test Panel with nested markup styles
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output

## Implementation Notes
- Check if Panel already parses markup correctly
- If not, may need to add Panel::from_markup() similar to Cell
- Add tests either way to document and lock down behavior

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs
- src/renderables/panel.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:50.977751990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036711554Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036694292Z","close_reason":"Created tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs with 14 tests covering: Panel::from_text() does not parse markup, title/subtitle do not parse markup, pre-styled Text preserves styles via from_rich_text(), nested styles work correctly, ANSI codes present in styled output, raw markup tags absent in pre-styled content. Documents that Panel behavior is consistent with Cell (markup not auto-parsed).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-25r","title":"Optimize Text::render to use byte slicing for zero-allocation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T01:33:23.896919865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T01:34:09.259210630Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T01:34:09.259168380Z","close_reason":"Optimized Text::render to slice string via byte indices","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2nv","title":"Targeted code review pass + file findings","description":"Perform focused code review for correctness/perf risks; file follow-up beads for any findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:53.973055499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601872493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601543252Z","close_reason":"Completed: filed findings bd-yb9 + bd-1wk","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
+{"id":"bd-3gpn","title":"Fresh-eyes audit pass: terminal detection + markup parser","description":"BV shows no open beads; creating a focused audit task. Scope: terminal detection (env var handling and color system selection) + markup parsing edge cases. Actions: read terminal.rs + markup/mod.rs end-to-end, trace callsites (Console usage), and fix any obvious behavioral bugs. Add regression tests where needed.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:22.322303363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:22.322303363Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-mg9","title":"Implement Renderable for primitives (str, String)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T02:06:42.562644012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:06:46.348244234Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T02:06:46.348203137Z","close_reason":"Implemented Renderable for str, String, Syntax, Markdown, Json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
